data.title: Canada probes Cigna's Express Scripts unit on antitrust concerns - report
data.description:
Canada's Competition Bureau said on Friday that it is investigating Cigna's pharmacy services unit Express Scripts on alleged anti-competitive conduct, according to a report by Reuters.

As per the report, the bureau is looking into Express Scripts' "patient steering" which makes people use pharmacies owned or associated with its Canada unit, instead of a drugstore of the patient's choice.

The agency is also investigating certain practices which are allegedly reducing margins of its pharmacy retailers.

Canada's Competition Bureau has ordered the company to produce records and other information, along with oral testimony related to the investigation, the report mentioned.

Seeking alpha has requested Cigna for a comment on the report.

data.title: Consumer sentiment sinks in April as inflation expectations jump
data.description:
April University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index: 50.8 vs. 54.0 consensus and 57.0 prior.

Current conditions: 56.5 vs. 61.5 consensus and 63.8 prior.

Consumer expectations: 47.2 vs. 50.8 consensus and 52.6 prior.

Year-ahead inflation expectations:  6.7% vs. 5.0% prior.

Five-year implied inflation: 4.4% vs. 4.1%.

Developing...check back for updates.

data.title: CoreWeave in spotlight as Hedgeye adds as new short idea
data.description:
• CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) was in the spotlight on Friday as Hedgeye added the recently public cloud service provider as a new short idea.
  • Shares fell 0.3% in early trading.
  • CoreWeave could be impacted amid concern about DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) impact to the artificial intelligence revolution, as well as its balance sheet, with “explosive” debt, the investment firm said.
  • Hedgeye added that CoreWeave's losses are likely to “greatly” accelerate and not peak until 2028, when they hit $2.8B. And it may not be profitable until 2032, at the earliest, the firm explained.
  • CoreWeave's business model is “highly dependent” upon Nvidia (NVDA) GPU demand, and the firm said that rental pricing for the H100 and H200 GPUs fell in the first-quarter of 2025. Additionally, Microsoft (MSFT), which is CoreWeave's largest client, may be pulling back on some of its data center investments, according to recent reports.
  • Other concerns include the potential for OpenAI to reduce its five-year, $11.9B deal with CoreWeave because of financial concerns of its own.

data.title: Dropbox's chief customer officer to depart, while activist investor woes linger
data.description:
Dropbox (NASDAQ:DBX) said its Chief Customer Officer Eric Cox plans to step down.

The move comes on the heels of activist investor Half Moon Capital being unhappy with the data storage company over its slowing revenue growth and its strategy on payment tiers. Last month, it was reported that, Half Moon, which owns about 40,000 shares of Dropbox, has submitted a proposal to remove the dual-class structure and get rid of CEO Drew Houston's voting supermajority.

The San Francisco-based company said in a regulatory filing that Cox will remain in his current role for a period of time to help with the transition, and then will continue as a non-executive employee through mid-August.

Dropbox did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha on Cox's departure and Half Moon Capital.

Shares of Dropbox dipped about 1% on Friday.

data.title: Blink Charging regains compliance with Nasdaq periodic filing requirement
data.description:
• Blink Charging (NASDAQ:BLNK) received a letter from The Nasdaq Stock Market on April 10, 2025 informing that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s periodic filing requirement.
  • On April 9, 2025, Blink filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024.
  • BLNK +6.56% premarket to $0.7383.
  • Source: Press Release
